steps.title: Translate the words into inequalities
steps.content: "At least 21" means $x \geq 21$. "Of the 27 scheduled meetings" caps the possible attendance at $27$, so $x \leq 27$.

steps.title: Combine into a compound inequality
steps.content: Both conditions hold at once, so $$21 \leq x \leq 27.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $x \le 21$ — allows values below the required minimum (e.g., $x=5$). Incorrect.
  • $x \ge 48$ — only $48$ meetings can't exist when just $27$ are scheduled. Incorrect.
  • The matching choice: $21 \le x \le 27$ — matches both "at least $21$" and the cap of $27$ scheduled meetings. Correct.
  • $27 \le x \le 48$ — forces $x$ above the total number of meetings. Incorrect.
C is correct.
